article_title: California Prepares to Launch Its First Carbon Capture and Storage Facility at Elk Hills This Spring

content_preview.title: California Prepares to Launch Its First Carbon Capture and Storage Facility at Elk Hills This Spring
content_preview.summary: Carbon TerraVault has completed construction of carbon capture equipment at Elk Hills and targets first CO2 injection in spring 2026, marking a milestone for California's carbon management ambitions.
content_preview.body_excerpt:
## Overview

California is on the verge of launching its first permitted carbon capture and storage operation. Carbon TerraVault, a subsidiary of California Resources Corporation, has completed construction of carbon capture equipment at the Elk Hills Cryogenic Gas Plant in Kern County and is now commissioning the facility ahead of a targeted first CO2 injection in spring 2026, according to a [company update published on March 2](https://www.globenewswire.com/news-release/2026/03/02/3247336/0/en...

editor_notes.content_quality: Well-structured News article at 544 words. Covers regulatory foundation, scale, partnerships, technical infrastructure, and investment outlook. Excellent use of primary sources (press releases and EPA announcement).
editor_notes.source_verification: All 3 sources verified. GlobeNewsWire (CRC update, March 2) — ✅ Confirmed: construction complete, spring 2026 target, 51%/49% CRC/Brookfield, 6.8 MMTPA MOUs, 352 MMT portfolio, $33M 2025 investment, $12-20M 2026 capex, $25-35M opex, Francisco Leon quote. GlobeNewsWire (Vallourec, March 9) — ✅ Confirmed: CLEANWELL technology, corrosion-resistant alloy pipe, first onshore CCS use, Chris Gould quote, California 2045 carbon neutrality. EPA announcement — ✅ Confirmed: four Class VI permits December 2024, first in California, first Pacific Southwest Region, depths exceeding one mile, Monterey Formation, 1.5M metric tons/year for 26 years (~38M total), 200 wells plugging requirement, $30M financial responsibility, Martha Guzman quote.
editor_notes.factual_accuracy: All claims verified against primary sources. Minor note: Vallourec press release states capacity as '1.46 million metric tons' while article says 'approximately 1.5 million' — EPA source confirms 1.5M, so the article's figure is correctly sourced. No hallucinations detected.
editor_notes.tone_assessment: Neutral and professional. Reports the project factually without either promoting CCS or criticizing it. Appropriately notes that injection 'remains subject to final EPA approval.'
editor_notes.originality: No existing carbon capture or CCS articles in the archive. Topic is entirely new.
editor_notes.overall_assessment: Excellent submission with all claims verified against primary sources. Strong use of official press releases and government announcements. Approved for publication.
